class.name: ImportInfo
class.brief_description: Metadata about an imported resource file in a loaded Godot project.
class.description: [ImportInfo] describes a single imported resource in a loaded Godot project. It stores the source file path, the engine-side destination paths, import parameters, and metadata read from [code].import[/code] files or embedded resource headers. GDRE uses [ImportInfo] objects internally to drive resource export - you can retrieve them from [GDRESettings] (via [method GDRESettings.get_import_info_by_source] or [method GDRESettings.get_import_files]) or load them directly from [code].import[/code] files with [method load_from_file]. [codeblock] var info = ImportInfo.load_from_file("res://textures/player.png.import", 4, 3) if info == null: push_error("Failed to load import info") return print("Source: ", info.source_file) print("Importer: ", info.get_importer()) print("Type: ", info.type) print("Dest files: ", info.dest_files) [/codeblock]

method.name: as_text
method.description: Returns a human-readable string representation of this import entry. Set [param full] to [code]false[/code] for a compact summary.

method.name: copy
method.description: Returns a deep copy of [param import_info].

method.name: from_json
method.description: Reconstructs an [ImportInfo] from a [Dictionary] in the format produced by [method to_json]. Returns [code]null[/code] if the dictionary is malformed.

method.name: get_compat_type
method.description: Returns the compatibility resource type string for this import entry. This is the resource type as it would be understood by the originating engine version, which may differ from Godot's current type for the same data.

method.name: get_iinfo_val
method.description: Returns the value of property [param p_prop] inside section [param p_section] of the [code].import[/code] file. Returns [code]null[/code] if the property does not exist.

method.name: get_iitype
method.description: Returns the internal type of this [ImportInfo] object - see [enum IInfoType] - indicating which import format it represents (Godot 2, modern Godot 3/4, remap, GDExtension, etc.).

method.name: get_importer
method.description: Returns the importer name used to import this resource (e.g. [code]"texture"[/code], [code]"ogg_vorbis"[/code], [code]"scene"[/code]).

method.name: get_param
method.description: Returns the value of the import parameter named [param key], as stored in the [code][params][/code] section of the [code].import[/code] file.

method.name: get_uid
method.description: Returns the UID string for this resource if one is stored in the import metadata, or an empty string otherwise.

method.name: get_ver_major
method.description: Returns the major engine version associated with this import entry.

method.name: get_ver_minor
method.description: Returns the minor engine version associated with this import entry.

method.name: has_dest_file
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if [param dest_file] is one of the engine-side destination paths produced by this import.

method.name: has_param
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if an import parameter named [param key] exists.

method.name: is_auto_converted
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if this resource was automatically converted by the Godot editor (for example, a text resource converted to binary at export time) rather than imported from an external source file.

method.name: is_import
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if this entry represents a proper imported resource (backed by a [code].import[/code] file or embedded import metadata), as opposed to a remap or a plain resource.

method.name: load_from_file
method.description: Loads import metadata from [param path] (a [code].import[/code] file or an imported resource path). Provide [param ver_major] and [param ver_minor] to disambiguate format differences between engine versions; pass [code]0[/code] to auto-detect. Returns [code]null[/code] on failure.

method.name: save_to
method.description: Writes the import metadata to a [code].import[/code] file at [param p_path]. Returns [code]OK[/code] on success.

method.name: set_iinfo_val
method.description: Sets the value of property [param p_prop] inside section [param p_section] of the [code].import[/code] file to [param p_val].

method.name: set_param
method.description: Sets the import parameter [param key] to [param value].

method.name: to_json
method.description: Serializes all import metadata to a [Dictionary], suitable for round-tripping with [method from_json].

members.member: Additional source files referenced by this import entry (e.g. normal-map companions for a texture importer).
The engine-side destination paths produced by the importer (e.g. [code].ctex[/code], [code].stex[/code] files).
The target output path that [ImportExporter] uses when recovering this resource.
Path to a lossless copy of the exported resource, if one is produced alongside a lossy export.
Path to the [code].import[/code] metadata file on disk.
Additional metadata properties stored alongside the import configuration (typically the [code][remap][/code] and other auxiliary sections).
All import parameters from the [code][params][/code] section of the [code].import[/code] file.
The preferred [code]res://[/code] path for this resource when exporting.
The [code]res://[/code]-rooted path to the original source file (e.g. [code]"res://textures/icon.png"[/code]).
MD5 checksum of the source file, as recorded in the [code].import[/code] file.

constants.constant: Loss type could not be determined.
Resource can be recovered without quality loss.
Resource is stored in a lossy format inside the pack.
Resource was imported with lossy settings.
Resource was imported with lossy settings [i]and[/i] stored in a lossy format.
Placeholder for resources with no real import data.
A resource remap entry (no external source file).
